data.title: Global market for Modular & Prefabricated Construction was $159.3B in 2024, expected to reach $203.8B by 2030, growing at 4.2% CAGR.
data.description: Dublin - A new report on Modular & Prefabricated Construction has been released by ResearchAndMarkets.com. The global market for this sector was valued at approximately US$159.3 billion in 2024 and is expected to increase to US$203.8 billion by 2030, with a projected compound annual growth rate of 4.2% during the forecast period from 2024 to 2030. This comprehensive analysis offers insights into market trends, drivers, and future forecasts to assist in making well-informed business decisions.

data.title: Trump hints at possible TikTok deal approval by China, but no specifics shared. Appreciates the nod in a call with Xi Jinping. Deal status remains uncertain.
data.description: The president expressed gratitude for the approval on Truth Social following a discussion with China's President Xi Jinping. However, he hinted that the deal and other matters are still being finalized.

data.title: HCM III Acquisition Corp. to start separate trading of Class A shares and warrants on Sept 22, 2025, on Nasdaq as "HCMA" and "HCMAW".
data.description: On September 19, 2025, HCM III Acquisition Corp. (NASDAQ: HCMAU) made an announcement that starting from September 22, 2025, individuals holding units from the company's initial public offering can choose to independently trade Class A ordinary shares and warrants from the units. It is important to note that only whole warrants will be traded as no fractional warrants will be issued during this process. The separated Class A ordinary shares and warrants will be available for trading on Nasdaq with the symbols "HCMA" and "HCMAW" respectively. Units that are not separated will continue to be traded on Nasdaq under the symbol "HCMAU."

data.title: Boeing's recovery accelerates with surge in aircraft deliveries for 737 MAX and 787 programs. Analyst predicts 118 units delivered in Q3, up from 104 in Q2 and 92 in Q3 last year. Price forecast of $270 with Buy rating.
data.description: Boeing Co.'s operational recovery is picking up speed, driven by a significant rise in aircraft deliveries for its key 737 MAX and 787 programs. This improvement on production lines suggests a potential turnaround in cash flow and overall financial performance for the aerospace giant. Analyst Ronald J. Epstein from Bank of America Securities has reiterated a Buy rating on Boeing Co. and set a $270 price target, citing steady deliveries and cash flow improvement as factors for anticipated stronger outcomes. The progress made on the 737 Max program in September is seen as a positive sign for the third quarter, with projections from Aero Analysis Partners indicating around 118 units to be delivered, surpassing the second quarter's 104 and last year's 92 deliveries. Epstein also emphasized the advancements in the 787 program.

data.title: Inventor creates PHANTOM X T device to pick up dog waste with ease, eliminating need to bend over.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on September 19, 2025, an inventor from Encino, California shared the creation of the PHANTOM X T, aimed at simplifying the pickup of dog feces with minimal effort. The inventor expressed the motivation behind the design, highlighting its ability to eliminate the task of bending over and handling pet waste or vomit from pets or children. The invention seeks to revolutionize this task with innovation, offering a convenient solution for pet owners.

data.title: Pimax reveals new VR headsets with widest FOV - Dream Air SE, Dream Air, and Crystal Super Micro-OLED. Launching soon.
data.description: In Wilmington, Delaware, on September 19, 2025, Pimax released a significant update about its Micro-OLED product range. This includes the Dream Air SE, Dream Air, and Crystal Super Micro-OLED headsets. Originally introduced earlier in the year, these devices are nearing their release date with confirmed specifications, and improved features.

data.title: HITN to premiere Spanish-language "Barcos de Esperanza," partnering with Esperanza US for event in Philadelphia.
data.description: In Brooklyn, New York, a recent premiere by HITN showcased the Spanish-language debut of "Barcos de esperanza" (Mercy Ships). The event was in partnership with Esperanza US and featured a screening in Philadelphia. The collaboration aimed to raise awareness about Mercy Ships and their philanthropic efforts. The initiative garnered attention as it highlighted the crucial work of Mercy Ships in providing medical assistance to underserved communities globally. This partnership underscores the importance of spreading the message in different languages to reach a wider audience.

data.title: A bullish AAVE price prediction aims for $358 by September 20, boosted by oversold RSI and a breakout potential at $331.83 resistance level.
data.description: The current AAVE price forecast indicates a bullish trend, with a projected target of $358 in the coming days. This prediction is supported by the oversold Relative Strength Index (RSI) showing signs of recovery and a key resistance level identified at $331.83, which, once broken, could lead to further upward movement in the price.

data.title: Bamboo Insurance grows, enters Texas homeowners market from California, a crucial expansion in its journey.
data.description: In Midvale, Utah, on September 19, 2025, Bamboo Insurance revealed its exciting entry into the Texas homeowners insurance sector. This significant step signifies a key achievement for the company, renowned for its substantial impact in California's highly competitive insurance landscape. This expansion opens doors to a vast market, with Texas boasting a sizeable number of homeowners and a promising business environment.
